title: Shawia lirata
taxonomy: Shawia lirata is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to the Macaronesian islands of the Atlantic Ocean. Shawia lirata is a succulent plant with fleshy leaves and stems. The plant's classification places it within the genus Shawia, which is a part of the tribe Boragineae. Further classification also includes it under the order Lamiales.

intro: Shawia lirata, also known as 'Dwarf or Rock Fig', is a small, evergreen shrub native to South Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may help in treating various ailments including wounds, infections, and oxidative stress-related conditions. The bioactive constituents of Shawia lirata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Shawia lirata is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat skin conditions, digestive issues, and as a topical application for wound healing.
